title: Rolling event window + bus subscription
predecessor: M33-T2 shipped 2026-04-16, commit 4ce9d3e (system-copilot pseudo-employee + role card + isSystemRoleId predicate + 4 unit tests; 1048 → 1052)
goal: Implement CopilotEventWindow — the in-memory rolling buffer that feeds T4's CopilotAnalyzerService. Per Phase 5 §8.5: a Map<companyId, bounded deque> keyed by company, holding the last N bus events (default MAX_EVENTS_PER_COMPANY = 100, locked private constant — NOT a setting in M33 — if Rocky wants it tunable later it gets a new settings key in M34+). Bus subscription receives every event from the append-only bus (same bus M31's agentic-tools and M29's RAG indexer subscribe to) and pushes onto the per-company deque; overflow drops the OLDEST event (FIFO eviction, preserving chronological prefix/suffix semantics for the analyzer). `snapshot(companyId): Event[]` returns a DEFENSIVE COPY (structuredClone or spread) — NEVER leak the internal deque reference because T4 reads it while the bus is still writing (race). Warm-start: on the FIRST `snapshot()` call per company, hydrate from `eventsRepo.listRecent(companyId, MAX_EVENTS_PER_COMPANY)` so a freshly-booted app with a week of history in the events table doesn't start the analyzer with an empty window. Hydration guards against double-seeding with a per-company `hydrated: Set<CompanyId>` flag. `clear(companyId)` wired to the `companies.archive` path so soft-deleting a company also drops its window (prevents memory leak on long-running sessions that archive + re-create companies). The class exports a single module-scoped singleton OR an instance injected by the composition root — follow the M31 AgenticLoopService pattern (composition-root instance) so tests can new up fresh instances per test.
acceptance: NEW apps/desktop/src/main/services/copilot-event-window.ts (or packages/intelligence/src/copilot/event-window.ts if the rolling window belongs with the analyzer — verify with Phase 5 §8.5 placement; default to apps/desktop for now since eventsRepo lives there and circular import risk is lower)

CopilotEventWindow class exposes: constructor(deps: { eventsRepo, busSubscribe }), push(companyId, event) (internal, bus-subscribed), snapshot(companyId): Event[] (public, returns defensive copy + hydrates warm-start on first call), clear(companyId): void (public, called from companies.archive)
MAX_EVENTS_PER_COMPANY = 100 as private const inside the module (NOT a setting in M33; document the rationale in an inline comment so future Loki ledger-searches find it)

Bus subscription fires on every event shape — do NOT filter by event kind in M33; T4's analyzer does kind-aware filtering when it builds the prompt. Filtering here would couple T3 to T4's categorization.

snapshot returns a DEFENSIVE COPY (per-element shallow clone or structuredClone of the array) — never the internal deque; add a unit test that mutates the returned array and asserts subsequent snapshot() calls are unaffected

Warm-start hydration: first snapshot() per company reads eventsRepo.listRecent(companyId, MAX_EVENTS_PER_COMPANY) and seeds the deque; guarded by a per-company `hydrated: Set<CompanyId>` flag so second snapshot() doesn't re-hydrate

clear(companyId) wired into companies.archive IPC handler (or the CompaniesRepo.archive method) — verify invariant #11 (IPC mutations emit bus events) doesn't regress by NOT removing existing bus emit, only ADD the clear() call

+8 unit tests net (1052 → 1060 target): bounds (push past 100 drops oldest — 3 tests: 99, 100, 101 items), company isolation (2 tests: pushing to A doesn't appear in B snapshot; clear(A) doesn't affect B), warm-start (2 tests: first snapshot hydrates from eventsRepo; second snapshot does NOT re-hydrate), archive clear (1 test: clear(companyId) empties window + next snapshot() re-hydrates)
Typecheck clean across all 6 workspace packages
Atomic commit: feat(m33): M33 T3 — rolling event window + bus subscription
Ledger commit: chore(loki): M33 T3 — commit ledger (<sha>)
filesTouched: apps/desktop/src/main/services/copilot-event-window.ts (NEW) — CopilotEventWindow class + MAX_EVENTS_PER_COMPANY const + bus subscription wiring
apps/desktop/src/main/services/copilot-event-window.test.ts (NEW) — 8 unit tests (bounds / isolation / warm-start / archive clear)

apps/desktop/src/main/db/repos/events.ts (verify listRecent(companyId, limit) method exists; add it if not — signature: listRecent(companyId: string, limit: number): Event[] ordered by created_at DESC)

apps/desktop/src/main/index.ts (composition root — instantiate CopilotEventWindow with eventsRepo + bus subscribe; keep behind a `copilotEventWindow` local so T4 can inject it into CopilotAnalyzerService)
apps/desktop/src/main/ipc/companies-handlers.ts OR apps/desktop/src/main/db/repos/companies.ts (wire clear(companyId) into the archive path; pick whichever owns the side-effect emit today)
Possibly packages/intelligence/src/copilot/event-window.ts if Phase 5 §8.5 placement prefers the intelligence package — check the plan doc before the first edit
notes: Placement decision: apps/desktop/src/main/services/ vs packages/intelligence/src/copilot/. Default apps/desktop/ because (a) eventsRepo + bus live there, (b) circular-import risk is lower, (c) T4's CopilotAnalyzerService (next task) will consume the window and can import from either side. Cross-check Phase 5 §8.5 before committing — if the plan explicitly places it in intelligence, move it and use a port/adapter pattern for the eventsRepo + bus to keep the package boundary clean.

Defensive copy rationale: the analyzer (T4) will iterate `window.snapshot(companyId)` while building its prompt — which takes 100–500ms on a cold LLM path. During that window the bus is still emitting. If snapshot returns the internal deque by reference, a push() overflow + shift() can mutate the array the analyzer is iterating — classic use-after-mutate. The defensive copy is cheap (100 events max, each event is <1KB) and eliminates the race. Document this in an inline comment for future contributors.

Warm-start hydration prevents the analyzer's first tick (post-reboot) from operating on an empty window. Without hydration, every app restart wipes the analyzer's short-term memory and a new app start on a company with no recent bus activity produces no insights for MAX_EVENTS_PER_COMPANY events. eventsRepo.listRecent is cheap (SQLite index on created_at) and hydrates in a single query per company per session.

The hydrated: Set<CompanyId> flag is critical — without it, a company that has <100 events in history and is actively producing new events will re-hydrate from eventsRepo on every snapshot() call, which is both wasteful AND stamps newer in-memory events with stale history ordering. Test this edge case explicitly.

Clear-on-archive semantics: companies.archive today soft-deletes (sets archived_at, preserves row). The window should also be cleared from memory on archive — otherwise a long-running session that archives + re-creates the same company id gets stale events in the re-created window. Align with M32's pattern where companies.archive triggers a cleanup of live services (verify by grepping for archive callers in the composition root).

NOT a setting in M33. MAX_EVENTS_PER_COMPANY is a locked private const. If Rocky asks to expose it as a setting, that's an M34 follow-up — add a note to orchestrator.json followUps at T10 rather than shipping a settings surface here.

Phase 5 invariant #3: the MCP host is a singleton in Main. CopilotEventWindow is NOT an MCP host but follows the same singleton-or-DI-instance posture — one instance per process, lifecycle-managed by the composition root. Don't accidentally spawn a second instance by importing the window class in both the analyzer AND a handler.

ABI rebuild dance for T3 unit-test verification: this task adds no native-module-touching code, but if you haven't run vitest since T2 the better-sqlite3 bindings may be stale (ABI 125 ≠ 127). Run `cd node_modules/.pnpm/better-sqlite3@11.10.0/node_modules/better-sqlite3 && npx node-gyp rebuild --release` BEFORE vitest; the Electron rebuild isn't needed for T3 because no E2E spec ships in this task.

Invariant #11 (IPC mutations emit bus events): ADD the clear() call to companies.archive WITHOUT removing the existing bus emit. Verify by grepping for `bus.emit` or `events.insert` in the archive handler/repo before and after the edit — the grep count must stay the same.
